expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the benchmark focus and enumerates what must be preserved during intake: it lists banding requirements (row/column, header-driven), style constraints (format colors), and rendering assumptions (visual striping/readability) under “Context intake must preserve (from provided evidence)”. It also notes Phase 1 does not draft scenarios: “Phase 1 does not draft scenarios.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: result.md frames the deliverable as Phase 1 spawn-manifest generation and validation: “Phase 1 is limited to generating a spawn manifest… Phase 1 does not draft scenarios.” The assessment is constrained to whether a phase1_spawn_manifest.json exists and what it would need to contain, and it marks the outcome “Inconclusive” due to the missing manifest rather than performing later-phase work.
